description: Structural eval scenarios. Expectations test output shape and gate behavior — not exact content, since live review data changes daily.

evals.description: High-signal productivity app. Notion iOS has 80k+ ratings and documented sync complaints. Expects multiple High-tier clusters, at least one broken promise detected, full copy-ready output.

broken_promise.note: Section 2 populated state depends on current store description and review distribution. Both states are valid; test only that Section 2 is present in some state.

evals.description: Finance app on Google Play. Tests the Google Play collection path, moderate complaint volume. Section 2 must be present in either populated or degraded state — never absent.

broken_promise.section_2_degraded: allowed — either populated table or one of the two degraded state notes
broken_promise.note: GP metadata fetch may or may not return a full description. Both states are valid.

evals.description: Tests Gate 1 (minimum 10 low-star reviews). Skill must stop before LLM analysis and output the diagnostic message. No output brief file should be created. Uses a mock fixture — see evals/fixtures/eval3-mock.json and evals/README.md.

input.note: Pass the fixture file as --input to fetch_reviews.py to simulate a low-signal app without a live URL.

clustering.note: No LLM clustering step should run

copy.note: No brief should be written to docs/review-briefs/
